message:
fix(error_renderer): emit serialized category enum instead of Debug-formatted string

ErrorRenderer::render_json and render_error_code_json were emitting the
category field using format!("{:?}", def.category), which produces the
Rust Debug representation (e.g. "Configuration", "InternalError") rather
than the serde-serialized form that every other JSON consumer in the
workspace expects for this enum.

The practical symptom is that JSON consumers reading `parsed["category"]`
see a Debug-case string that does not match the enum's `#[serde(...)]`
discriminants — so any downstream pipeline filtering on `category ==
"configuration"` (or whatever the serde-attribute rename emits) will
quietly miss every event.

Fix by passing `def.category` directly into `serde_json::json!(...)`,
which invokes the enum's Serialize impl and produces the canonical JSON
form — matching every other code path that serializes the same enum via
serde_json::to_value / to_string.

Applied at two call sites:
  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/output/error_renderer.rs render_json
  - crates/frankenterm-core/src/output/error_renderer.rs render_error_code

## Regression coverage

Two new tests pin the contract so a future refactor cannot slip back to
Debug-format:

  - render_json_category_uses_serialized_error_category: render a real
    Error (WeztermError::CliNotFound -> FT-1001), parse the output JSON,
    and assert parsed["category"] equals
    serde_json::to_value(get_error_code("FT-1001").unwrap().category)
    — i.e. whatever the enum's Serialize impl emits, not a string literal.
    This stays correct even if the enum's serde renames change in the
    future because the assertion is authored against the same
    serialization path the code uses.

  - render_error_code_json_category_uses_serialized_error_category: same
    invariant for the render_error_code convenience entry point.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
